author: Player3
content:
feat(theory): Phase Q — two QA archetypes; the end-to-end discrete-sourced (non-unit) QA
Builds the discrete-sourced QA end-to-end (from Phase P) and pins the precise structural fact:
the two orders live in COMPLEMENTARY COMPLETIONS of the global field, and 'which place is the
source' = 'which completion the order is local to'. qa_two_archetypes.py (axiom-linter clean,
Codex-approved), 2x2 locality proof:

  GOLDEN QA (unit generator phi): word is STURMIAN -- n-th letter = [{n/phi} >= 1-1/phi],
    verified 0 mismatches -> ARCHIMEDEAN-local; and exists n with fib_n != fib_(n+2^10) ->
    NOT 2-adic-local. Order lives at the ARCHIMEDEAN (observer) completion (real precision of
    phi). Entropy log phi, module Z[phi]. mod-m does NOT determine it (why Phase L destroys it).
  DISCRETE-SOURCED QA (non-unit generator 2, period-doubling): word is 2-AUTOMATIC -- n-th
    letter = [v_2(n) even] exactly -> 2-adic-LOCAL; best archimedean threshold still misses 33%
    -> NOT archimedean-local. Order lives at the p-ADIC completion (the mod-2^k tower = QA's A1
    finite-alphabet layer at its natural limit). Entropy log 2, module Z[1/2]. NO real number,
    NO observer projection needed to generate it.

NET: there are TWO QA archetypes, selected by the generator's UNIT-NESS (Phase M): a UNIT
generator sites the order at the ARCHIMEDEAN (observer) completion (golden, quasicrystal,
Sturmian, irrational frequency); a NON-UNIT generator sites it at the p-ADIC (discrete A1)
completion (automatic, limit-periodic, rational frequency). The discrete-sourced QA is real and
end-to-end: for a non-unit generator the QA's own finite arithmetic IS the source of a genuine
aperiodic order, the archimedean readout an optional derived shadow -- the exact reverse of the
golden QA.

Honest consequence for the framework: Theorem NT holds the discrete layer primary and continuous
functions to be observer readouts. For the GOLDEN QA that is in tension -- its characteristic
order (the quasicrystal) is observer-sited (archimedean), which is exactly why mod-m destroys it.
A NON-UNIT QA resolves the tension: its order is genuinely native to the discrete mod-p^k layer.
Both archetypes are shadows of the one global adelic object; the generator's unit-ness picks which
completion (observer vs discrete) hosts the order. This is a precise structural fact, not a flaw.

Answers the inversion question opened by Phase O: can the DISCRETE (finite) layer be the

qa_firewall_polarity.py (axiom-linter clean, Codex-approved). Two aperiodic pure-point-

  FIBONACCI (golden): inflation [[1,1],[1,0]], eigenvalues phi,psi = UNITS (N=-1); STURMIAN
    (n-th letter is a cut of a line of slope 1/phi, a real number); letter frequency 1/phi,

    is NOT automatic -> Fibonacci cannot be built from finite-place digits. SOURCE = the
    OBSERVER (archimedean) place. (This is the whole golden arc A->O.)
  PERIOD-DOUBLING: inflation [[1,2],[1,0]], eigenvalues 2,-1 -> 2 is a NON-unit (N=-2); its
    n-th letter is EXACTLY [v_2(n) even] -- verified for 20000 positions -- a function of the
    2-ADIC valuation of n ALONE (base-2 digit data, NO real number); frequency 2/3, RATIONAL;

Cross-check confirms non-interchangeable sources: the 2-adic rule [v_2 even] does NOT produce
Fibonacci, and Fibonacci's irrational frequency bars it from any base-p automaton.

NET: the Theorem-NT firewall polarity INVERTS, and the unit/non-unit dichotomy (Phase M) IS
the polarity. UNIT inflation (golden phi) => order at the OBSERVER (archimedean) place --
irrational-frequency, non-automatic, needs a real number. NON-unit inflation (period-doubling
factor 2) => order at the DISCRETE (finite/2-adic) place -- rational-frequency, automatic,
limit-periodic, built from base-2 digits with NO real number, and NATIVE to QA's mod-2^k A1
layer with the real positions as the DERIVED shadow. So QA's discrete layer is not only a
passive shadow of the observer projection: for non-unit inflations it is the SOURCE of a whole
QA-native family of aperiodic (automatic / limit-periodic) orders that the golden/unit thread
structurally cannot reach. Entropy: golden log phi (archimedean); period-doubling log 2.

author: Player3
content:
feat(theory): Phase O — the projection's SOURCE and NT violations made precise
Answers "the projection layer has to project from somewhere; what are the NT violations
specifically?" qa_nt_violations.py (axiom-linter clean, Codex-approved):

WHERE the projection projects FROM: Theorem NT names the observer<->discrete firewall but was
silent on the domain. The source is the GLOBAL Z[phi]/icosian element (the diagonal point in
the adele of Q(sqrt5)). A single global element phi^k = F_{k-1}+F_k*phi has TWO exact
archimedean shadows -- sigma1=phi^k (physical) and sigma2=psi^k (the contracting WINDOW, the
term a Binet float drops) -- and one exact finite shadow (the residue). Observer (archimedean)
and QA-discrete (finite) are SIBLING shadows of the one global object; neither is prior.

WHAT an NT violation IS, specifically -- all ONE thing: producing a finite-place (discrete)
value that is NOT the reduction of a global object (place-mixing without a global source):
  * T2-b float x modulus -> int cast (DEMONSTRATED): casting the observer's Binet float
    readout phi^k/sqrt5 mod m agrees only by luck of rounding, then float64 precision fails at
    k=71 and it gives WRONG residues -- it is not a ring homomorphism, cannot carry the exact
    residue the global integer's reduction does.
  * T2   observer output -> QA input (feedback): re-injecting a readout as a discrete input.
  * T2-D continuous/stochastic -> QA: an archimedean measure as QA state (no finite counterpart).
  * S2   float QA state: storing the discrete object as an archimedean approximation.
Legitimacy TEST for an archimedean SELECTOR (e.g. a cut-and-project window): is it the global
object's own Z[phi]-shadow? M's window [psi,1) is M-inflation-invariant (legitimate); an
external transcendental window [1/pi,1) is not (a violation). A discrete input is legal iff it
is the reduction of a Z[phi] element (a ring homomorphism).

NET: the firewall is crossed legitimately ONLY through the global object. Reduction Z[phi] ->
Z[phi]/m (a ring homomorphism) is a projection; casting/copying an archimedean shadow into a
finite place is a violation, because the two shadows are tied by the product formula (Phase N),
not by identity. This names the domain Theorem NT was missing and gives an exact, testable line
between projection and violation.

(Codex caught: the exact archimedean shadow of the integer F_k is F_k itself; phi^k/sqrt5 is
only the Binet approximation -- rewrote [A] to show the exact global element phi^k in Z[phi]
with its two exact real embeddings sigma1=phi^k, sigma2=psi^k, the float entering only as the
approximation whose cast then fails. And the axiom linter blocked writing the T2-b pattern
literally -- fittingly enforcing the very firewall being characterized -- so the demonstration
relies on the k=71 divergence rather than executing the cast.)

feat(theory): Phase N (capstone) — the adelic conservation law that closes the arc A->N
Reads Phase M's product formula dynamically and closes the loop back to Phase A.
qa_adelic_balance.py (axiom-linter clean, Codex-approved):

  [A] ONE number, three guises (all = log phi = 0.4812): the Lyapunov exponent of the QA
      golden dynamics (Phase A cat map), the topological entropy of M as a hyperbolic toral
      automorphism (= log spectral radius), and the logarithmic MAHLER MEASURE of x^2-x-1
      (= sum log+ |roots|). Benettin-measured Lyapunov 0.4812 confirms it. The mod-m dynamics
      is Pisano-periodic (periods 10/14/50) -> topological entropy 0. So the unpredictability
      (entropy = log phi) lives at the expanding ARCHIMEDEAN (observer) place; the discrete
      A1 (mod-m) layer is periodic, entropy 0, predictable.

  [B] product formula (additive sum_v log|psi|_v = 0) for the golden UNIT psi: the two
      archimedean places carry -log phi (window/contract, the observer projection) and
      +log phi (physical/expand) and CANCEL; every finite place is 0 (psi is a unit). The
      balance is ENTIRELY between the two observer places; the discrete layer is NEUTRAL --
      exactly WHY the golden order & entropy are observer-only (unifying Phases A, L, M).

  [C] for a NON-unit (root of x^2-x-11, N=-11): archimedean places sum to +log 11, the FINITE
      (discrete) places sum to -log 11 -- the discrete layer PARTICIPATES. So UNITS decouple
      observer & discrete; NON-UNITS couple them.

NET: the Theorem-NT firewall (observer <-> discrete, crossed exactly twice) IS the split of
the adeles of Q(sqrt5) into archimedean (observer: physical + window) and finite (discrete:
the mod-m A1 layer) places; the product formula is the conservation law across it. For QA's
golden M (a unit), observer and discrete DECOUPLE -- the observer place carries the whole
entropy (log phi) AND the whole quasicrystalline order, while the discrete layer is entropy-0,
periodic, neutral. That is the number-theoretic bedrock under the entire determinism->
quasicrystal arc, and it closes the loop back to Phase A's Lyapunov ln phi.

author: Player3
content:
feat(theory): Phase M — golden order is archimedean-only (no finite carrier); observer = 2nd real place
Resolves the arc's final frontier (from Phase L): is the psi-contraction window a strictly
OBSERVER/archimedean object, or can a finite/p-adic modulus carry it? qa_padic_window.py
(axiom-linter clean, Codex-approved):

  [A] The golden window has NO finite carrier -- PROVABLY, because phi/psi are UNITS.
      N(psi)=psi*phi=-1, so |psi|_v=1 at EVERY finite place v (valuation 0). By the product
      formula the whole contraction lives at the two ARCHIMEDEAN (real) places:
      |psi|_s1=0.618 (<1, the contracting internal/window) x |psi|_s2=|phi|=1.618 (>1,
      expanding) = 1. Verified psi stays a nonzero invertible residue (v_p=0, no contraction)
      at split primes 11/19/29/31. So the acceptance window = the SECOND archimedean place =
      the Galois-conjugate real embedding = the Theorem-NT OBSERVER projection. This is the
      exact reason every mod-m attempt (Phase H, Phase L) failed: golden order is the
      archimedean Pisot property, strictly observer-layer.

  [B] But discrete aperiodic order is not impossible -- only the GOLDEN one is barred (phi is
      a unit / Pisot = archimedean). A NON-unit recurrence x^2-x-p has a root of p-adic
      valuation 1 (Newton polygon), which CONTRACTS p-adically. Measured mod 11^8: the
      golden internal coordinate stays a p-adic unit (max valuation 0, in no p-adic ball),
      while the non-unit internal coordinate reaches valuation 7 (-> 0 p-adically, confined
      to p^7 Z) -- even though its ARCHIMEDEAN reach is 93% (the two metrics genuinely differ;
      it is the p-adic one that carries the window). The non-unit confinement (internal == 0
      mod p^k) IS a mod-p^k condition, so its aperiodic/limit-periodic order is definable
      mod p^k: a genuine DISCRETE carrier, which the golden M is not.

NET: the observer projection (Theorem NT) is identified precisely as the SECOND ARCHIMEDEAN
place of Q(sqrt5) / the Pisot conjugate. Golden order is archimedean-only, provably no finite
carrier (unit). 'Aperiodic order can be discrete' (yes -- non-golden, p-adic, non-unit) is
cleanly separated from 'the GOLDEN order can be discrete' (no). This is the sharp end of the
whole determinism->quasicrystal arc: the golden/M/sqrt5 order lives at the archimedean
observer place; the QA discrete A1 (finite-place) layer cannot carry it, and now we have the
number-theoretic proof of why (phi/psi are units).

Codex-caught refinement: judged p-adic confinement by the p-adic VALUATION, not the
archimedean reach (a p-adically tiny number can be archimedean-large in [0,p^K)); the
survives-test now uses valuation, and the metric distinction is stated explicitly.

The golden element M=[[0,1],[1,1]] is BOTH the QA orbit generator AND the Fibonacci INFLATION
matrix; its two eigenvalues supply BOTH cut-and-project spaces from its own eigenstructure:
  phi (expanding) -> PHYSICAL space; psi=-1/phi (contracting) -> INTERNAL space = the
  acceptance WINDOW, which is the GALOIS CONJUGATE coordinate = the Theorem-NT OBSERVER
  projection (the contracting/internal direction).

  [1] cut-and-project Z^2 through M's eigenspaces (window=[psi,1), the unit-cell projection)
      -> the Fibonacci chain: exactly 2 tiles, ratio phi, 91% match to the L->LS,S->L
      substitution word (same chain as Phase I).
  [2] M acts as the INFLATION: x_par->phi*x_par, x_perp->psi*x_perp (verified); the model set
      is a self-similar FIXED POINT of iterating M (the golden orbit).
  [3] the QA golden orbit (F_k,F_{k+1}) internal coord = -psi^k*(2+psi)/sqrt5 -> 0 (0.382 ->
      4e-5): the contraction CONFINES the orbit to the window -> the QA orbit IS a model set.
  [4] mod-m (the A1 finite-alphabet layer) DESTROYS it, MEASURED: over Z the internal coord
      stays bounded (max 0.382, a window). Mod 11/29/101, phi/psi survive only as F_m

      Weil order, not golden.

NET: QA dynamics DOES generate the quasicrystal -- in the UNREDUCED (observer-layer, Z[phi])
M-inflation -- and the acceptance window = internal space = psi = Galois conjugate = the
Theorem-NT observer projection, unifying the firewall with the golden/quasicrystal arc. The
mod-m QA layer is exactly the "measurement" that collapses it. So this reinforces (not
overturns) the earlier finding: the golden order is an observer-layer phenomenon; QA's
discrete A1 layer collapses it -- and now we know precisely why (loss of the psi contraction).

Codex caught two real issues, both fixed: (1) destroyed.append(True) was unconditional
(validation theater) -> replaced with a measured, falsifiable reach-of-internal-coordinate
test; (2) prose said "no phi/psi in F_m" but the chosen primes split x^2-x-1, so psi_m does
exist -- corrected to "phi/psi survive as residues but lose archimedean magnitude, so the
contraction (not the eigenvalue) is what's destroyed".

      icosahedral group 2I (order 120); via q->R_q they give exactly 60 icosahedral

      The exact isometry E8 ~= icosian ring is Elser-Sloane 1987 / Conway-Sloane SPLAG
      Sec.8.2 -- cited, not re-derived (no faked projection matrix).
  [C] COMPUTED: the 3D icosahedral module from the 6 golden five-fold axes is
      ICOSAHEDRALLY INVARIANT (axis-set closure 1.000 under all 60 rotations -- exact,
      not dense-set rounding), admits the crystallographically FORBIDDEN 5-fold axis

icosian ring. Observer-layer (Theorem NT) but exact and published. Figure:

(3/2)NkT + pairwise potential of bodies moving through space) -- wrong register for the
plenum. In the FST/plenum picture energy is the COHERENCE of the virtual flux, and QA's
own coupling einsum('ik,jk->ij',T,T) is a coherence functional (inner products = phase
alignment). qa_coherence_energy.py (axiom-linter clean, Codex-approved) redoes the
holographic DOF-scaling question with that definition: C(N)=|sum_{k<=N} exp(2i*pi*s_k/m)|^2
for the golden orbit, exponent C~N^beta = coherent-DOF count (beta=1 volume/extensive,
2/3 area/holographic, 0 bounded, 2 bulk-coherent). No virial, no imposed voxels.

Hardened to avoid theater: probed a FULL Pisano period (n_max=2e6 ~ pi(P)=2_000_008, not a
2% transient), built a real incoherent null (beta=0.98+/-0.14 over 120 seeds; method check
PASS: null~1, aligned~2), added a large-N tail check and non-golden algebraic controls
(geometric g^k mod P, sqrt13 recurrence) to separate golden-special from generic number
theory. Verdict is computed from the data, not asserted.

RESULT (data-driven): QA golden orbit mod P has full-range beta=0.88/0.91/0.92 (coords
b/d/a) but this is NOT robustly sub-extensive: z=-0.5 (p=0.30, within the wide null),
and the large-N TAIL beta=1.51 != 0.91 -> pre-asymptotic transient, not a scaling law.
And it is NOT golden-specific: non-golden geometric (0.64) and sqrt13 (0.68) do the same
-- the generic Weil ~sqrt(P) cancellation of ANY algebraic sequence mod a prime; phi is
not doing the work. The one clean bounded (sub-extensive) case is the CONTINUUM golden
ANGLE (beta=0.03), a property of the observer-layer irrational angle that discretization
mod P does not inherit (QA mod P -> 0.9, not 0.03).

NET: BOTH energy definitions agree with the arc -- re-defining energy as coherence does
not give QA a native sub-extensive/holographic scaling any more than the mechanical
translation did. QA is the discrete substrate; the sub-extensive (holographic/
gravitational) scaling is a separate ingredient QA is compatible with but does not supply.
My first quick run (n_max=40k) showed a spurious beta~0.76 "signal" that evaporated under
full-period probing + a proper null + the tail check -- recorded as a caught artifact.

Scaffolds cert family [522] certifying that cert [518]'s distortion-correction
operator qa_neg (the standard involution = phase conjugation = TIME REVERSAL) run
IN REVERSE FOCUSES energy back onto a source through a scattering medium (Fink's
time-reversal mirror), instead of correcting distortion at a receiver.

Mechanism certified deterministically (integer-only, no external data):
- FOCUS_CONSTANT: matched re-emission makes the field at the source constant across
  all array elements (= qa_neg(s)) -> maximally coherent focus;
- MEDIUM_CANCEL: that constant is qa_neg(s) independent of H (verified, 2 media);
- OFF_SOURCE_SCATTER: a distinct off-source location's field is not constant;
- SPECIFICITY: re-emitting through a different medium H' breaks the focus -- the
  [518] same-medium fingerprint, now for focusing.
Ref impl qa_time_reversal_focus.py (m=24, 64-element): refocuses exactly at the
source (peak 1.000), matched-vs-mismatched medium gap +0.756. Honest limit: 1D
finite-aperture sidelobes (~0.49 background). 2 PASS + 3 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok.

Primary: Fink (1992) DOI:10.1109/58.156174; Prada & Fink (1994); Yariv (1978).
Builds on [518],[519]; phase-conjugation cluster companions [520],[521].
Registered in qa_meta_validator.py (ast.parse OK, [522] PASS via registry fn).

feat(svp): QA time-reversal focusing — the distortion-correction operator in reverse
Phase 4 (empirical pivot) opener, prompted by "phase conjugation / distortion
correction can be done in reverse and be useful, yes?" -- yes. qa_time_reversal_focus.py.

The phase-conjugate operator qa_neg is the standard involution (adjugate) from the
quaternion grounding = optical phase conjugation = TIME REVERSAL. Cert [518] used it
to CORRECT distortion at a receiver; deployed the other way it FOCUSES energy back
onto a source through a scattering medium (medical ultrasound / seismic / acoustic
time-reversal mirrors). Demo (m=24, 64-element array):
- refocuses exactly at the source through an arbitrary phase screen (peak 1.000 at
  x0; H cancels analytically because H*H* = identity -- verified);
- SAME-MEDIUM SPECIFICITY (the [518] fingerprint, now for focusing): matched medium
  peak 1.000 vs mismatched 0.244, gap +0.756 -- focus only through the matched medium.

Same operator, reversed use. Extends the phase-conjugation cluster [518]-[521];
cert-ready. A1/S2/Theorem-NT: integer phases; geometry+medium observer-in, coherent-
sum focus metric observer-out.

content:
docs(theory): consolidate the quaternion grounding into one authoritative narrative
Phase 2 of the post-quaternion plan (write-up). Restructures
QA_AS_QUATERNION_ORDER.md from a chronological session log ("direction 2 - done",
"the one real open program ... not a one-session close") into a clean top-down
document a mathematician can read start to finish. No new claims; same verified
content, reordered and de-diaried:

  1. QA = M2(Z), M the golden element (M^2=M+I) -> why orbits are Fibonacci
  2. the order is maximal; versor grade parity (M is nrd=-1, not in B^1=SL(2,Z))
  3. unification of the versor [294-303] and orbit [384-431] clusters
  4. integrality / axiom S2 (fractional (b,e) leaves the order)
  5. the three special primes de-conflated (5 ramifies Z[phi], 3 inert, M2 split)
  6. Brandt/Hecke: the split-algebra obstruction -> the definite order over Q(sqrt5)
     = icosian ring = E8 -> the level-125 Eichler order + its Brandt matrices,
     computed from scratch, reproducing the full Hecke system of 2.2.5.1-125.1-a
  7. what is verified + primary sources

Fixes the now-obsolete "open program" framing (the Brandt/Hecke bridge is closed)
and the stale check count (qa_brandt_level125.py is 16/16). Status v1 -> v2.

content:
feat(theory): verify the full Hecke system — all good primes to norm 100 match LMFDB
Phase 1 (math hardening) of the post-quaternion plan. Extends the Brandt
computation from 3 sample primes to EVERY good prime up to norm 100 (13 primes):
stage [7] of qa_brandt_level125_compute.sage computes T(q) for each and checks the
cusp factor (S,P) against the LMFDB eigenvalue system of 2.2.5.1-125.1-a. All 13
match exactly -- the whole L-function (to that bound) is reproduced from the
quaternion side, not just three samples. qa_brandt_level125.py now 16/16.

Split primes p==1 mod5 give the nonzero Galois-conjugate pairs (e.g. p11: x^2+x-31,
p31: x^2+11x-1, p41: x^2-9x-11, p71: x^2-19x+59); every other good prime is
CM-inert (a_q=0) with cusp factor x^2. #neighbours = N(q)+1 throughout (Eisenstein).
